nutrient_source.title: Red bean (adzuki) paste dessert / bean jelly — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Adzuki beans, cooked (FDC ID 175188) and sugar, granulated (FDC ID 169655), combined with agar-agar (FDC ID 168869), adjusted for typical Chinese red bean jelly cake formulation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for palace-style Chinese red bean jelly cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Values are estimated from component ingredients: cooked adzuki beans (~55% by weight), sugar (~20%), agar-agar (~2%), and water (~23%), weighted and blended per standard formulation. Energy calculated using Atwater factors. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of adzuki beans and red bean-based products — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (American Diabetes Association peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 29 is based on adzuki beans (cooked) as reported in the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The palace-style red bean jelly cake contains cooked adzuki beans as the primary carbohydrate source. Added sugar raises the effective GI slightly above plain cooked beans (GI ~28–29), but the high fibre and protein content of beans moderates the overall glycemic response. GI estimated at 29 (low). GL per 180 g serving = (29 × 32.5 × 1.80) / 100 = approximately 17.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~18 mg per 100 g); potassium from adzuki beans supports blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 18 mg sodium per 100 g and a meaningful potassium content (~210 mg per 100 g), this dessert poses minimal sodium-related risk for individuals managing hypertension, though portion size should be monitored due to added sugar content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load (32.5 g per 100 g) with low GI (29) due to adzuki bean fibre and protein; added sugar is a consideration
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycemic index (GI 29) driven by adzuki bean fibre and protein moderates postprandial glucose response, but the glycemic load per standard 6-piece serving (~17) is moderate, and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size and account for the added sugar (~18 g per 100 g) within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.1 g per 100 g), zero cholesterol, and soluble fibre from adzuki beans support cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dessert is very low in saturated fat and cholesterol-free; the soluble dietary fibre from adzuki beans (~2.8 g per 100 g) may contribute modestly to LDL cholesterol reduction, making it a relatively heart-friendly dessert choice when consumed in moderate portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red Bean Cake (宫廷小豆凉糕, Gōngtíng Xiǎodòu Liánggāo) is a traditional Chinese palace-style chilled dessert made primarily from cooked adzuki (red) beans, sugar, agar-agar or gelatin, and water. The preparation involves simmering adzuki beans until soft, blending or mashing them with sugar and a setting agent, pouring the mixture into moulds, and chilling until firm. The result is a smooth, lightly sweet, cooling jelly-like cake with a soft, yielding texture and a subtle earthy bean flavour. The dessert is described as 'cooling' in the context of traditional Chinese dietary philosophy, making it popular in warm climates such as Singapore. It is served in portions of 6 pieces and is notable for being lightly sweet rather than cloying. Nutritionally, it provides moderate carbohydrates (~32.5 g per 100 g) with a low glycemic index of approximately 29, driven by the high fibre and protein content of adzuki beans. It is very low in fat (0.3 g per 100 g) and saturated fat (0.1 g per 100 g), and contains meaningful amounts of potassium (~210 mg), iron (~1.2 mg), folate (~60 mcg DFE), and magnesium (~28 mg) per 100 g. The dessert is naturally gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal-suitable. Clinically, the low GI and low saturated fat profile make it a relatively favourable dessert option for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease, though the added sugar content warrants portion awareness for diabetic individuals. The glycemic load per standard 6-piece serving (180 g) is approximately 17, which is moderate.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for palace-style Chinese red bean jelly cake was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted component ingredients (cooked adzuki beans, sugar, agar-agar, water) using USDA SR Legacy data. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (cooked adzuki beans) per Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Confidence is medium due to the component-estimation approach rather than a direct food match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cooked adzuki beans (FDC ID 175188), granulated sugar (FDC ID 169655), and agar-agar (FDC ID 168869), blended at approximate formulation weights (55% beans, 20% sugar, 2% agar, 23% water). Direct laboratory analysis of this specific product is not available in any approved source database. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed trace/negligible per USDA adzuki bean data; effectively zero by formulation). polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.1 g (small amount from adzuki beans). trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero; no hydrogenated fats in this formulation). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero; plant-based dessert with no animal-derived fat). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero; heat processing destroys residual vitamin C in cooked beans). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero; plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients). 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this formulation in the source database. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this plant-based dessert. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this formulation. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for adzuki bean jelly cake. 4. GI of 29 is an analogous estimate based on cooked adzuki beans per Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care International GI Tables). GL per 180 g serving calculated as (29 × 32.5 × 1.80) / 100 ≈ 17.0. 5. The food is sold as '6 Pcs' at SGD 15.60 by See Ya Tonight 今晚有局 on field survey data. Serving size estimated at 180 g (6 × ~30 g pieces). 6. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03315671 and FoodEx2 A00LC are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology code exists for this specific product. SNOMED CT has no suitable concept. 7. Dietary flags: vegan and vegetarian status assumes agar-agar (not gelatin) is used as the setting agent, consistent with the traditional palace-style formulation and the vegan/halal-suitable description. If animal gelatin is used, vegan and halal flags would require revision.
